message:
fix(fcp-bitbucket): enforce base_url endpoint policy at configure boundary (resolves br-3a2ce)

Previously `BitbucketConfig::from_params` accepted any base_url and the
connector constructed a `BitbucketClient` (carrying access-token /
app-password credentials) BEFORE any policy check fired —
`provisioning_readiness` reported the rejection but did not fail
configuration, so a malicious config could route bearer credentials to
an attacker-controlled host such as `https://bitbucket.example.com/2.0`.

Promote the existing `base_url_policy` check to a hard refuse inside
`from_params`. If the base_url is not (https + api.bitbucket.org /
bitbucket.org host) and not a localhost/127.0.0.1/::1 test allowance,
return `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003, message: <policy text> }`.
The `BitbucketClient` (and its bearer-token plumb-through) is never
constructed for an off-policy URL.

Same pattern as br-898o4 (zapier, 63493e6e) and br-y9qr5 (teams, 9a2aabd2).

Regression tests:
  * config_rejects_off_policy_https_host: refuses
    https://bitbucket.example.com/2.0, asserts the rejection names the
    bitbucket allowlist
  * config_rejects_http_non_local_host: refuses
    http://api.bitbucket.org/2.0 (downgrade attempt)
  * config_accepts_localhost_for_tests: locks in the local-test-host
    allowance so the policy fix never breaks tests/integration.rs
    wiremock fixtures
  * config_default_base_url_is_within_policy: regression guard for
    DEFAULT_BASE_URL drift

The previous `config_custom_base_url` test was the bug — it asserted
that `https://bitbucket.example.com/2.0` was accepted. Renamed and
inverted to `config_custom_base_url_within_policy` using
`https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0` so the policy-conforming custom-URL
path stays exercised.

206 fcp-bitbucket lib + 39 integration tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
